1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حم
1.english: Ha, Meem.
1.transliteration: Haa-Meeem

1.arabic: تنزيل الكتاب من الله العزيز الحكيم
1.english: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
1.transliteration: Tanzeelul Kitaabi minal laahil 'Azeezil Hakeem

1.arabic: إن في السماوات والأرض لآيات للمؤمنين
1.english: Indeed, within the heavens and earth are signs for the believers.
1.transliteration: Innaa fis samaawaati wal ardi la Aayaatil lilmu'mineen

1.arabic: وفي خلقكم وما يبث من دابة آيات لقوم يوقنون
1.english: And in the creation of yourselves and what He disperses of moving creatures are signs for people who are certain [in faith].
1.transliteration: Wa fee khalaqikum wa maa yabussu min daaabbatin Aayaatul liqawminy-yooqinoon

1.arabic: واختلاف الليل والنهار وما أنزل الله من السماء من رزق فأحيا به الأرض بعد موتها وتصريف الرياح آيات لقوم يعقلون
1.english: And [in] the alternation of night and day and [in] what Allah sends down from the sky of provision and gives life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness and [in His] directing of the winds are signs for a people who reason.
1.transliteration: Wakhtilaafil laili wannahaari wa maaa anzalal laahu minas samaaa'i mir rizqin fa ahyaa bihil arda ba'da mawtihaa wa tasreefir riyaahi Aayaatul liqawminy ya'qiloon

1.arabic: تلك آيات الله نتلوها عليك بالحق ۖ فبأي حديث بعد الله وآياته يؤمنون
1.english: These are the verses of Allah which We recite to you in truth. Then in what statement after Allah and His verses will they believe?
1.transliteration: Tilka Aayatul laahi natloohaa 'alika bilhaqq, fabiayyi hadeesim ba'dal laahi wa Aayaatihee yu'minoon

1.arabic: ويل لكل أفاك أثيم
1.english: Woe to every sinful liar
1.transliteration: Wailul likulli affaakin aseem

1.arabic: يسمع آيات الله تتلى عليه ثم يصر مستكبرا كأن لم يسمعها ۖ فبشره بعذاب أليم
1.english: Who hears the verses of Allah recited to him, then persists arrogantly as if he had not heard them. So give him tidings of a painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Yasma'u Aayaatil laahi tutlaa 'alaihi summa yusirru mustakbiran ka-al lam yasma'haa fabashshirhu bi'azaabin aleem

1.arabic: وإذا علم من آياتنا شيئا اتخذها هزوا ۚ أولئك لهم عذاب مهين
1.english: And when he knows anything of Our verses, he takes them in ridicule. Those will have a humiliating punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa 'alima min Aayaatinaa shai'anit takhazahaa huzuwaa; ulaaa'ika lahum 'azaabum muheen

1.arabic: من ورائهم جهنم ۖ ولا يغني عنهم ما كسبوا شيئا ولا ما اتخذوا من دون الله أولياء ۖ ولهم عذاب عظيم
1.english: Before them is Hell, and what they had earned will not avail them at all nor what they had taken besides Allah as allies. And they will have a great punishment.
1.transliteration: Minw waraaa'ihim Jahannamu wa laa yughnee 'anhum maa kasaboo shai'anw wa laa mat takhazoo min doonil laahi awliyaaa'a wa lahum 'azaabun 'azeem

1.arabic: هذا هدى ۖ والذين كفروا بآيات ربهم لهم عذاب من رجز أليم
1.english: This [Qur'an] is guidance. And those who have disbelieved in the verses of their Lord will have a painful punishment of foul nature.
1.transliteration: Haazaa hudanw wal lazeena kafaroo bi aayaati Rabbihim lahum 'azaabum mir rijzin aleem

1.arabic: ۞ الله الذي سخر لكم البحر لتجري الفلك فيه بأمره ولتبتغوا من فضله ولعلكم تشكرون
1.english: It is Allah who subjected to you the sea so that ships may sail upon it by His command and that you may seek of His bounty; and perhaps you will be grateful.
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee sahkhara lakumul bahra litajriyal fulku feehi bi amrihee wa litabtaghoo min fadlihee wa la'allakum tashkuroon

1.arabic: وسخر لكم ما في السماوات وما في الأرض جميعا منه ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يتفكرون
1.english: And He has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth - all from Him. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.
1.transliteration: Wa sakhkhara lakum maa fis samaawaati wa maa fil ardi jamee'am minh; inna feezaalika la Aayaatil liqawminy yatafakkaroon

1.arabic: قل للذين آمنوا يغفروا للذين لا يرجون أيام الله ليجزي قوما بما كانوا يكسبون
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], to those who have believed that they [should] forgive those who expect not the days of Allah so that He may recompense a people for what they used to earn.
1.transliteration: Qul lillazeena aamanoo yaghfiroo lillazeena laa yarjoona ayyaamal laahi liyajziya qawmam bimaa kaanoo yaksiboon

1.arabic: من عمل صالحا فلنفسه ۖ ومن أساء فعليها ۖ ثم إلى ربكم ترجعون
1.english: Whoever does a good deed - it is for himself; and whoever does evil - it is against the self. Then to your Lord you will be returned.
1.transliteration: Maa 'amila saalihan falinafsihee wa man asaaa'a fa'alaihaa summa ilaa Rabbikum turja'oon

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا بني إسرائيل الكتاب والحكم والنبوة ورزقناهم من الطيبات وفضلناهم على العالمين
1.english: And We did certainly give the Children of Israel the Scripture and judgement and prophethood, and We provided them with good things and preferred them over the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Baneee Israaa'eelal Kitaaba walhukma wan Nubuwwata wa razaqnaahum minat taiyibaati wa faddalnaahum;alal 'aalameen

1.arabic: وآتيناهم بينات من الأمر ۖ فما اختلفوا إلا من بعد ما جاءهم العلم بغيا بينهم ۚ إن ربك يقضي بينهم يوم القيامة فيما كانوا فيه يختلفون
1.english: And We gave them clear proofs of the matter [of religion]. And they did not differ except after knowledge had come to them - out of jealous animosity between themselves. Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
1.transliteration: Wa aatainaahum baiyinaatim minal amri famakh talafooo illaa mim ba'di maa jaaa'ahumul 'ilmu baghyam bainahum; inna Rabbaka yaqdee bainahum Yawmal Qiyaamati feemaa kaanoo feehi yakhtalifoon

1.arabic: ثم جعلناك على شريعة من الأمر فاتبعها ولا تتبع أهواء الذين لا يعلمون
1.english: Then We put you, [O Muhammad], on an ordained way concerning the matter [of religion]; so follow it and do not follow the inclinations of those who do not know.
1.transliteration: Summa ja'alnaaka 'alaa sharee'atim minal amri fattabi'haa wa laa tattabi'ahwaaa'al-lazeena laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: إنهم لن يغنوا عنك من الله شيئا ۚ وإن الظالمين بعضهم أولياء بعض ۖ والله ولي المتقين
1.english: Indeed, they will never avail you against Allah at all. And indeed, the wrongdoers are allies of one another; but Allah is the protector of the righteous.
1.transliteration: Innahum lany yughnoo 'anka minal laahi shai'aa; wa innaz zaalimeena ba'duhum awliyaaa'u ba'dinw wallaahu waliyyul muttaqeen

1.arabic: هذا بصائر للناس وهدى ورحمة لقوم يوقنون
1.english: This [Qur'an] is enlightenment for mankind and guidance and mercy for a people who are certain [in faith].
1.transliteration: Haazaa basaaa'iru linnaasi wa hudanw wa rahmatul liqawminy yooqinoon

1.arabic: أم حسب الذين اجترحوا السيئات أن نجعلهم كالذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات سواء محياهم ومماتهم ۚ ساء ما يحكمون
1.english: Or do those who commit evils think We will make them like those who have believed and done righteous deeds - [make them] equal in their life and their death? Evil is that which they judge.
1.transliteration: Am hasibal lazeenaj tarahus saiyiaati an naj'alahum kallazeena aamanoo wa 'amilu saalihaati sawaaa'am mahyaahum wa mamaatuhum; saaa'a maa yahkumoon

1.arabic: وخلق الله السماوات والأرض بالحق ولتجزى كل نفس بما كسبت وهم لا يظلمون
1.english: And Allah created the heavens and earth in truth and so that every soul may be recompensed for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.
1.transliteration: Wa khalaqal laahus samaawaati wal arda bilhaqqi wa litujzaa kullu nafsim bimaa kasabat wa hum laa yuzlamoon

1.arabic: أفرأيت من اتخذ إلهه هواه وأضله الله على علم وختم على سمعه وقلبه وجعل على بصره غشاوة فمن يهديه من بعد الله ۚ أفلا تذكرون
1.english: Have you seen he who has taken as his god his [own] desire, and Allah has sent him astray due to knowledge and has set a seal upon his hearing and his heart and put over his vision a veil? So who will guide him after Allah? Then will you not be reminded?
1.transliteration: Afara'ayta manit takhaza ilaahahoo hawaahu wa adal lahul laahu 'alaa 'ilminw wa khatama 'alaa sam'ihee wa qalbihee wa ja'ala 'alaa basarihee ghishaawatan famany yahdeehi mim ba'dil laah; afalaa tazakkaroon

1.arabic: وقالوا ما هي إلا حياتنا الدنيا نموت ونحيا وما يهلكنا إلا الدهر ۚ وما لهم بذلك من علم ۖ إن هم إلا يظنون
1.english: And they say, "There is not but our worldly life; we die and live, and nothing destroys us except time." And they have of that no knowledge; they are only assuming.
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo maa hiya illaa hayaatunad dunyaa namootu wa nahyaa wa maa yuhlikunaaa illad dahr; wa maa lahum bizaalika min 'ilmin in hum illaayazunnoon

1.arabic: وإذا تتلى عليهم آياتنا بينات ما كان حجتهم إلا أن قالوا ائتوا بآبائنا إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: And when Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, their argument is only that they say, "Bring [back] our forefathers, if you should be truthful."
1.transliteration: Wa izaa tutlaa 'alaihim aayaatuna baiyinaatim maa kaana hujjatahum illaaa an qaalu'too bi aabaaa'inaaa in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: قل الله يحييكم ثم يميتكم ثم يجمعكم إلى يوم القيامة لا ريب فيه ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون
1.english: Say, "Allah causes you to live, then causes you to die; then He will assemble you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt, but most of the people do not know."
1.transliteration: Qulil laahu yuhyeekum summa yumeetukum summa yajma'ukum ilaa Yawmil Qiyaamati laa raiba feehi wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: ولله ملك السماوات والأرض ۚ ويوم تقوم الساعة يومئذ يخسر المبطلون
1.english: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And the Day the Hour appears - that Day the falsifiers will lose.
1.transliteration: Wa lillaahi mulkus samaawaati wal ard; wa Yawma taqoomus Saa'atu Yawma 'iziny yakhsarul mubtiloon

1.arabic: وترى كل أمة جاثية ۚ كل أمة تدعى إلى كتابها اليوم تجزون ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: And you will see every nation kneeling [from fear]. Every nation will be called to its record [and told], "Today you will be recompensed for what you used to do.
1.transliteration: Wa taraa kulla ummatin jaasiyah; kullu ummatin tud'aaa ilaa kitaabihaa al Yawma tujzawna maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: هذا كتابنا ينطق عليكم بالحق ۚ إنا كنا نستنسخ ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: This, Our record, speaks about you in truth. Indeed, We were having transcribed whatever you used to do."
1.transliteration: Haazaa kitaabunaa yantiqu 'alaikum bilhaqq; innaa kunnaa nastansikhu maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: فأما الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات فيدخلهم ربهم في رحمته ۚ ذلك هو الفوز المبين
1.english: So as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is what is the clear attainment.
1.transliteration: Fa ammal lazeena aamaanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati fayudkhiluhum Rabbuhum fee rahmatih; zaalika huwal fawzul mubeen

1.arabic: وأما الذين كفروا أفلم تكن آياتي تتلى عليكم فاستكبرتم وكنتم قوما مجرمين
1.english: But as for those who disbelieved, [it will be said], "Were not Our verses recited to you, but you were arrogant and became a people of criminals?
1.transliteration: Wa ammal lazeena kafarooo afalam takun Aayaatee tutlaa 'alaikum fastakbartum wa kuntum qawmam mujrimeen

1.arabic: وإذا قيل إن وعد الله حق والساعة لا ريب فيها قلتم ما ندري ما الساعة إن نظن إلا ظنا وما نحن بمستيقنين
1.english: And when it was said, 'Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth and the Hour [is coming] - no doubt about it,' you said, 'We know not what is the Hour. We assume only assumption, and we are not convinced.' "
1.transliteration: Wa izaa qeela inna wa'dallaahi haqqunw was Saa'atu laa raiba feehaa qultum maa nadree mas Saa'atu in nazunnu illaa zannanw wa maa nahnu bimustaiqineen

1.arabic: وبدا لهم سيئات ما عملوا وحاق بهم ما كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: And the evil consequences of what they did will appear to them, and they will be enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
1.transliteration: Wa badaa lahum saiyiaatu maa 'amiloo wa haaqa bihim maa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: وقيل اليوم ننساكم كما نسيتم لقاء يومكم هذا ومأواكم النار وما لكم من ناصرين
1.english: And it will be said, "Today We will forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours, and your refuge is the Fire, and for you there are no helpers.
1.transliteration: Wa qeelal yawma nansaakum kamaa naseetum liqaaa'a yawmikum haazaa wa maawaakumun Naaru wa maa lakum min naasireen

1.arabic: ذلكم بأنكم اتخذتم آيات الله هزوا وغرتكم الحياة الدنيا ۚ فاليوم لا يخرجون منها ولا هم يستعتبون
1.english: That is because you took the verses of Allah in ridicule, and worldly life deluded you." So that Day they will not be removed from it, nor will they be asked to appease [Allah].
1.transliteration: Zaalikum bi annakumut takhaztum aayaatil laahi huzuwanw wa gharratkumul hayaatud dunyaa; fal yawma laa yukhrajoona minhaa wa laahum yusta'taboon

1.arabic: فلله الحمد رب السماوات ورب الأرض رب العالمين
1.english: Then, to Allah belongs [all] praise - Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Falillaahil hamdu Rabbis samaawaati wa Rabbil ardi Rabbil-'aalameen

1.arabic: وله الكبرياء في السماوات والأرض ۖ وهو العزيز الحكيم
1.english: And to Him belongs [all] grandeur within the heavens and the earth, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
1.transliteration: Wa lahul kibriyaaa'u fissamaawaati wal ardi wa Huwal 'Azeezul Hakeem
